exercises.name: Agility Ladder Icky Shuffle
exercises.simplyPut: Sharpen your stability and coordination with Agility Ladder Icky Shuffle. This intermediate-level balance exercise challenges your legs while training your proprioceptive system.

mistakes.error: Progressing too fast (eyes closed before mastering eyes open)
mistakes.correction: Master each progression before advancing: bilateral → tandem → single leg → eyes closed
Always within arm's reach of wall, chair, or railing; spotter for advanced work

mistakes.consequence: Disrupts vestibular and visual balance systems; worsens balance
mistakes.correction: Fix gaze on stationary point at eye level; use peripheral vision for foot placement
Flat-soled shoes or barefoot (supervised) for maximum proprioceptive feedback

physiologyLinks.effect: Dual-task balance training improves executive function
Mindful balance practices (tai chi) reduce cortisol 10-20%
Tai chi reduces falls by 43% in elderly (Cochrane review)
Balance training improves joint position sense by 20-40%

pre_workout.why_avoid: Full stomach affects center of gravity. Alcohol and sedatives impair proprioception.

during_workout.why_avoid: Balance training requires focus — GI activity is distracting.

post_workout.recovery_note: B-vitamins support nervous system recovery. Magnesium aids neuromuscular function.

foodsPositive.benefit: Flavanols improve cerebral blood flow and reaction time
foodsPositive.mechanism: Epicatechin increases NO in cerebral vasculature, improving vestibular processing

foodsPositive.benefit: DHA for brain-muscle coordination + calcium for bone density
foodsPositive.mechanism: DHA integrates into neuronal membranes, improving synaptic transmission speed

foodsPositive.benefit: Healthy fats support myelin sheath integrity for nerve conduction
foodsPositive.mechanism: Oleic acid incorporated into myelin membrane, maintaining signal speed

foodsPositive.benefit: Anthocyanins improve neural signaling for proprioception
foodsPositive.mechanism: Cross blood-brain barrier, enhancing BDNF expression and cerebellar function

foodsNegative.impairment: Low blood sugar impairs cerebellar function — balance decreases
foodsNegative.mechanism: Brain requires glucose; hypoglycemia reduces cerebellar processing speed

foodsNegative.impairment: Drowsiness reduces reaction time during balance challenges
foodsNegative.mechanism: Tryptophan crosses BBB, increasing serotonin and melatonin precursors
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: SSRIs + tryptophan-heavy meal = serotonin syndrome risk (rare)

foodsNegative.impairment: Impairs vestibular function and proprioception — fall risk
foodsNegative.mechanism: Ethanol alters endolymph density in semicircular canals, disrupting balance signals

exercises.name: Flat Floor Bilateral Stance (Static)
exercises.simplyPut: Sharpen your stability and coordination with Flat Floor Bilateral Stance (Static). This beginner-level balance exercise challenges your legs while training your proprioceptive system.

mistakes.error: Progressing too fast (eyes closed before mastering eyes open)
mistakes.correction: Master each progression before advancing: bilateral → tandem → single leg → eyes closed
Always within arm's reach of wall, chair, or railing; spotter for advanced work

mistakes.consequence: Disrupts vestibular and visual balance systems; worsens balance
mistakes.correction: Fix gaze on stationary point at eye level; use peripheral vision for foot placement
Flat-soled shoes or barefoot (supervised) for maximum proprioceptive feedback

physiologyLinks.effect: Mindful balance practices (tai chi) reduce cortisol 10-20%
Balance training improves joint position sense by 20-40%
Tai chi reduces falls by 43% in elderly (Cochrane review)
Dual-task balance training improves executive function

pre_workout.why_avoid: Full stomach affects center of gravity. Alcohol and sedatives impair proprioception.

during_workout.why_avoid: Balance training requires focus — GI activity is distracting.

post_workout.recovery_note: B-vitamins support nervous system recovery. Magnesium aids neuromuscular function.

exercises.name: Single Leg Stand
exercises.simplyPut: Stand on one foot and hold your balance for as long as possible. It sounds embarrassingly simple, but this exercise reveals hidden weaknesses in ankle stability, hip strength, and core control. Fall prevention in elderly adults starts with this one exercise.
exercises.howTo: Perform Single Leg Stand with focus on stability and body awareness.

mistakes.error: Progressing too fast (eyes closed before mastering eyes open)
mistakes.correction: Master each progression before advancing: bilateral → tandem → single leg → eyes closed
Always within arm's reach of wall, chair, or railing; spotter for advanced work

mistakes.correction: Fix gaze on stationary point at eye level; use peripheral vision for foot placement

exercises.name: Tai Chi: Wave Hands Like Clouds
exercises.simplyPut: Slowly shift your weight from side to side while your arms float through the air in gentle circular motions, like parting clouds. Tai Chi is meditation in motion — this movement improves balance, reduces stress, and is one of the most evidence-based exercises for fall prevention in elderly adults.

mistakes.correction: Master each progression before advancing: bilateral → tandem → single leg → eyes closed
Always within arm's reach of wall, chair, or railing; spotter for advanced work

mistakes.consequence: Disrupts vestibular and visual balance systems; worsens balance
mistakes.correction: Fix gaze on stationary point at eye level; use peripheral vision for foot placement
Flat-soled shoes or barefoot (supervised) for maximum proprioceptive feedback

physiologyLinks.effect: Balance training improves joint position sense by 20-40%
Tai chi reduces falls by 43% in elderly (Cochrane review)
Mindful balance practices (tai chi) reduce cortisol 10-20%
Dual-task balance training improves executive function

pre_workout.why_avoid: Full stomach affects center of gravity. Alcohol and sedatives impair proprioception.

during_workout.why_avoid: Balance training requires focus — GI activity is distracting.

post_workout.recovery_note: B-vitamins support nervous system recovery. Magnesium aids neuromuscular function.

foodsPositive.benefit: Anthocyanins improve neural signaling for proprioception
foodsPositive.mechanism: Cross blood-brain barrier, enhancing BDNF expression and cerebellar function

foodsPositive.benefit: Healthy fats support myelin sheath integrity for nerve conduction
foodsPositive.mechanism: Oleic acid incorporated into myelin membrane, maintaining signal speed

foodsPositive.benefit: DHA for brain-muscle coordination + calcium for bone density
foodsPositive.mechanism: DHA integrates into neuronal membranes, improving synaptic transmission speed

foodsPositive.benefit: Flavanols improve cerebral blood flow and reaction time
foodsPositive.mechanism: Epicatechin increases NO in cerebral vasculature, improving vestibular processing

foodsNegative.impairment: Impairs vestibular function and proprioception — fall risk
foodsNegative.mechanism: Ethanol alters endolymph density in semicircular canals, disrupting balance signals

foodsNegative.impairment: Low blood sugar impairs cerebellar function — balance decreases
foodsNegative.mechanism: Brain requires glucose; hypoglycemia reduces cerebellar processing speed

foodsNegative.impairment: Drowsiness reduces reaction time during balance challenges
foodsNegative.mechanism: Tryptophan crosses BBB, increasing serotonin and melatonin precursors
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: SSRIs + tryptophan-heavy meal = serotonin syndrome risk (rare)
